field.value: The image of Athena (Minerva) in an unusual pose, with her right hand happened to hold a large serpent. To understand the iconography, we need to keep in mind that the sculpture was heavily restored in the seventeenth century by Alessandro Algardi, who completed the statue found in fragments in the Campus Martius in 1627, integrating the hand and lower body of the goddess and the serpent. The statue is a copy of an ancient original work of art. The signature of the copyist, the Athenian sculpture Antiokos or Metiokos, belonging to the Neo-Attic school, is still legible on the lower part of the statue, although doubts remain about its authenticity.
